title: Foreign IBAN (non-DE/AT/CH)
text: Genuine German Inkasso companies almost always present a German (DE), Austrian (AT), or Swiss (CH) IBAN. A foreign IBAN — particularly Polish (PL), Czech (CZ), Slovak (SK), Latvian (LV), or other — on a German-language Inkasso demand is one of the strongest fraud indicators. Money sent to a foreign IBAN is virtually unrecoverable.

title: Private-person recipient instead of registered company
text: Legitimate Inkasso companies are licensed under the Rechtsdienstleistungsgesetz (RDG) and registered as companies. A recipient name that looks like a natural person (e.g. 'Herr Maximilian Schmidt') rather than a registered company (e.g. 'XYZ Inkasso GmbH, eingetragen im Handelsregister Berlin HRB 12345') is a critical fraud indicator.

title: Legal threats (Schufa, Gerichtsvollzieher) in a first letter
text: German consumer law requires a graduated escalation: invoice → reminder (Mahnung) → debt-collection notice → court order (Mahnbescheid) → bailiff (Gerichtsvollzieher). Threats of Schufa reporting, Gerichtsvollzieher, or 'gerichtliche Schritte' in a FIRST letter — without prior Mahnung documentation — is a critical fraud indicator. Real Inkasso companies follow the legal sequence.

title: Extreme 24-72 hour deadline
text: Standard payment deadlines in German Inkasso are 14 days (zwei Wochen). Deadlines of 24, 48, or 72 hours are designed to prevent the recipient from verifying the demand. An extreme deadline is one of the four critical-tier fraud indicators in the Verbraucherzentrale taxonomy.

title: Missing regulatory information
text: Legitimate Inkasso companies must display: company name, registered office, court of registration (Amtsgericht) + commercial-register number (HRB), authorised representatives, RDG registration number, supervisory authority (Aufsichtsbehörde — usually the Oberlandesgericht). Absence of one or more of these is a supporting fraud indicator.

title: Unclear or unnamed creditor
text: A legitimate Inkasso must name the original creditor (Gläubiger) plus the contractual basis for the claim (e.g. 'Mobilfunkvertrag mit Vodafone vom 03.04.2024'). A vague or missing creditor is a supporting fraud indicator.

title: Dubious or surprising fees
text: Inkasso fees are capped by the Rechtsanwaltsvergütungsgesetz (RVG) — typically €30-100 plus statutory interest. Unusual line items (e.g. 'Bearbeitungsgebühr €200', 'Express-Versandkosten €50') beyond the RVG schedule are supporting fraud indicators.

title: Phantom company names
text: Names that combine generic German legal terms ('Forderungsmanagement', 'Inkassodienst', 'Rechtsabteilung') without a verifiable handelsregister entry. Cross-check via Verbraucherzentrale Schwarzliste Inkasso or unternehmensregister.de.

title: Premium phone numbers
text: Telephone numbers beginning with 0900, 0180 (premium-rate), or +49-900 prefixes — especially as the ONLY contact method — are supporting fraud indicators. Legitimate Inkasso companies offer landline + email contacts.

title: Pressure tactics ('letzte Mahnung' as first letter)
text: Letters labeled 'LETZTE MAHNUNG' (final reminder), 'SOFORT' (immediately), 'DRINGEND' (urgent) when the recipient has received no prior correspondence with the alleged creditor. Pressure-tactic vocabulary in a first contact is a supporting fraud indicator.

title: Three-level classification rule
text: legitimate: zero indicators OR only 1 supporting indicator with verifiable creditor + RDG company + DE/AT/CH IBAN → no UI change. suspicious: ≥2 supporting indicators OR 1 critical indicator with mitigating context → yellow advisory + verification step prepended. likely_scam: ≥1 critical indicator OR ≥3 supporting indicators → red banner + recommendations completely overridden to 'do not pay, verify via Verbraucherzentrale, save as evidence'.

title: Verbraucherzentrale Schwarzliste Inkasso
text: The Verbraucherzentrale Brandenburg maintains a public Schwarzliste (blacklist) of confirmed fake-Inkasso companies + name-and-address combinations. Volume context (per Verbraucherzentrale public reporting): ~191,000 page views in a single year; 400 reported fake-Inkasso letters in 2022 alone. The Schwarzliste is the authoritative source for confirming a specific company name as fraudulent.

title: Why fake Inkasso targets refugees
text: Refugees (especially recent arrivals) are disproportionately targeted because: language barriers prevent verification through official channels; unfamiliarity with German consumer-protection institutions (Verbraucherzentrale, Aufsichtsbehörde); financial stress makes pressure tactics more effective; the German postal address registration system (Anmeldung) makes addresses easily harvestable. The German consumer-protection establishment recommends multilingual outreach + bureaucracy-translation tools targeted at protected groups.
